title: Case Changing
subtitle: ~ flips one character. gU/gu/g~ are operators.
summary: ~ toggles the case of the character under the cursor. gU is an uppercase operator; gu is lowercase; g~ toggles. Each takes a motion: gUiw uppercases the inner word.

blocks.text: Three operators for case: {key:g}{key:U} uppercases, {key:g}{key:u} lowercases, {key:g}{key:~} toggles. Each takes a motion, just like {key:d} and {key:y}. {key:~} alone is a one-shot toggle of the character under the cursor.

blocks.title: More Grammar Showing Through
blocks.text:
{key:g}{key:U}, {key:g}{key:u}, {key:g}{key:~} are operators by the same pattern as {key:d}, {key:c}, {key:y}, {key:>}. Every motion or text object pairs:

  • {key:g}{key:U}{key:G} — uppercase to end of file.
  • {key:g}{key:U}{key:a}{key:p} — uppercase a paragraph.
  • {key:g}{key:U}{key:a}{key:'} — uppercase the contents of a single-quoted string (including the quotes).
  • {key:g}{key:U}{key:U} — uppercase the line (the doubled form).

{key:~} (single tilde) is one-shot like {key:x}: it works on a character and accepts a count. {key:5}{key:~} toggles the case of the next 5 chars.

`'tildeop'` makes {key:~} *itself* into an operator (so {key:~}{key:i}{key:w} would toggle inner word). Off by default — {key:g}{key:~}{key:i}{key:w} is the consistent way.
